framework.name: Act on the Development of Artificial Intelligence and Establishment of Trust Foundation
framework.issuing_body: Ministry of Science and ICT (Republic of Korea)
framework.scope: This act establishes South Korea’s national AI governance framework, including a presidential AI committee, policy support bodies, learning-data policies, trust and safety measures, and rules for high-impact and generative AI. It applies broadly to AI activities affecting the domestic market or users, including some extraterritorial conduct, while excluding AI developed and used solely for national defense or national security as prescribed by Presidential Decree.
framework.applies_to: AI developers, providers, and business operators whose activities affect the Korean market or Korean users, including foreign operators above applicable thresholds; the Act also creates special obligations for high-impact AI, generative AI, and AI systems meeting the prescribed computation threshold. State and local governments, designated committees, policy centers, and safety institutions are also addressed for governance and support functions.
framework.penalties: Enforcement includes Ministerial fact-finding investigations and cease/correct orders under Article 40. Article 42 provides criminal punishment of up to 3 years’ imprisonment or a fine up to KRW 30 million for breaching committee confidentiality, and Article 43 provides administrative fines up to KRW 30 million for certain notice, domestic-agent, and order-compliance failures.

control.statement: Determine and document whether AI activities fall within the Act’s territorial and subject-matter scope, including extraterritorial impacts and national defense/security exclusions.
Provide prior notice and required labeling for high-impact AI and generative AI outputs, including AI-generated sound, images, and video where applicable.

Implement lifecycle AI safety risk identification, assessment, mitigation, and management controls for AI systems meeting the prescribed computation threshold, and submit implementation results to the Minister.
Perform a prior review to determine whether an AI system is high-impact and retain evidence supporting the classification decision.
For high-impact AI, maintain operator responsibilities covering risk management, explanation plans, human oversight, user protection, and required records.
Conduct or endeavor to conduct a high-impact AI impact assessment and retain the assessment outputs and any related prioritization evidence for public-sector procurement.
If a foreign AI business operator meets the threshold, designate and report a domestic agent within the required timeframe.
Respond to fact-finding investigations and, where required, submit data and comply with cease or correction orders for suspected violations of transparency, safety, or high-impact AI duties.
Protect committee confidentiality and prohibit disclosure or misuse of confidential information obtained through committee service.
Avoid administrative-fine-triggering failures, including missing required notices, missing domestic-agent designation, or failing to comply with cease/correct orders.

source.title: Korean Law Information Center — Act on the Development of Artificial Intelligence and Establishment of Trust Foundation
Korea Policy Briefing — AI Basic Act support desk
Korean Law Information Center — enforcement decree
